strand: Materials and physical processes (floating, melting, movement)
gapNote: No EYFS app exploring everyday materials, floating/sinking or melting through play
title: Float or Sink Tank
concept: Drag everyday objects into a water tank and watch them float or sink, then sort them into two hoops as a class prediction game

strand: Care for and respect the natural environment
gapNote: No app on caring for living things or the local environment
title: Look After the Garden
concept: Children tap to water, weed and feed birds in a garden scene, learning how to care for living things and the environment

strand: Animals including humans (nutrition, skeletons, digestion, circulation, life cycles)
gapNote: No KS2 app on the human body: digestive system, skeleton/muscles, teeth, or the circulatory system
title: Journey Through Digestion
concept: Send food on an animated trip through mouth, stomach and intestines, tapping each organ to learn its job

strand: States of matter and the water cycle
gapNote: No KS2 app on solids/liquids/gases, melting/freezing or evaporation and the water cycle (KS3 Matter app is pitched higher)
title: Heat It, Cool It
concept: Drag a thermometer up and down to melt, freeze, evaporate and condense a substance, watching particles spread out and pack in

strand: Light and shadows
gapNote: No KS2 app on light travelling in straight lines, reflection or shadow formation (EM Spectrum app is KS3)
title: Shadow Studio
concept: Move a light source around an object and watch the shadow change size and direction, with a sundial challenge

strand: Sound (vibration, pitch, volume)
gapNote: No app on how sound is made by vibrations, travels, and changes pitch/volume
title: See the Sound
concept: Pluck strings and bang drums to watch vibrations and a live waveform, with sliders for pitch and volume

strand: Earth and space (solar system, day/night, Moon phases)
gapNote: No KS2 app on the heliocentric solar system, Earth's rotation causing day/night, or Moon phases
title: Spin the Earth
concept: Rotate the Earth and orbit it round the Sun to show day/night and the year, with a movable observer experiencing sunrise

strand: Evolution and inheritance (adaptation, variation, fossils as evidence)
gapNote: No KS2 app on adaptation, inherited variation or evolution evidence (Punnett app is KS4 genetics)
title: Survive the Island
concept: Change an environment (cold, predators) and select which beak/fur traits survive over generations to show adaptation

strand: Working scientifically at KS2 (variables, fair tests, recording, conclusions)
gapNote: No dedicated enquiry-skills app for planning fair tests, choosing variables, taking measurements and drawing conclusions
title: Fair Test Designer
concept: Set up an investigation by choosing the variable to change, what to measure and what to keep the same, then auto-plot the results

strand: Chemistry: particulate nature of matter and states (particle model, changes of state, diffusion)
gapNote: Local KS3 file lists Matter (particle model, Brownian motion, diffusion, pressure) but no app simulates particles across states
title: Particle Theatre
concept: Heat and cool a substance and watch particles change arrangement and energy across solid, liquid and gas, with pressure and diffusion demos

strand: Chemistry: chemical reactions, energetics and the Earth/atmosphere
gapNote: No KS3 app on word/symbol equations, conservation of mass, acids and bases, exothermic/endothermic, or the carbon cycle/atmosphere
title: Reaction Balancer
concept: Drag atoms to balance an equation and weigh products to prove conservation of mass with a live mass readout

strand: Physics: space (gravity, solar system, seasons, the universe)
gapNote: Local KS3 file details Space Physics (g, weight, solar system, seasons/tilt, light year) but no app covers it (Electric Fields is KS5)
title: Weight on Other Worlds
concept: Drop an object on different planets, changing g, and compare weight vs mass with a live W = m g readout

strand: Working scientifically (planning, apparatus, analysis, evaluation)
gapNote: No KS3 enquiry-skills app for variables, accuracy/precision, drawing/interpreting graphs and evaluating method
title: Investigation Studio
concept: Plan a method, choose apparatus and variables, collect simulated data with random error, then plot and evaluate reliability

strand: Biology: homeostasis and response (nervous/hormonal control)
gapNote: No app on the nervous system/reflex arc, blood glucose control, the menstrual cycle hormones or thermoregulation
title: Blood Sugar Balance
concept: Eat and exercise and watch insulin/glucagon return blood glucose to normal, modelling type 1 and 2 diabetes

strand: Chemistry: bonding, structure and properties of matter
gapNote: No app on ionic/covalent/metallic bonding, dot-and-cross diagrams or structure-property links
title: Bonding Builder
concept: Drag electrons to form ionic and covalent bonds, generate dot-and-cross diagrams and predict the resulting properties

strand: Chemistry: quantitative chemistry (moles, equations, calculations)
gapNote: No app on conservation of mass, the mole, reacting masses, concentration or percentage yield
title: Mole Calculator Lab
concept: Balance an equation then convert between mass, moles and particles with a live reacting-mass and yield calculator

strand: Chemistry: chemical changes and energy changes
gapNote: No app on acids/bases/neutralisation, reactivity series, electrolysis or exothermic/endothermic profiles
title: Electrolysis Cell
concept: Electrolyse molten and aqueous solutions, watch ions migrate and predict products at each electrode

strand: Chemistry: chemical analysis, atmosphere and using resources
gapNote: No app on purity/chromatography, gas/flame tests, the evolving atmosphere/climate change or sustainable resources
title: Chromatography and Tests
concept: Run paper chromatography, calculate Rf and perform flame and gas tests to identify unknown samples

strand: Biology: response, homeostasis and control (nervous, hormonal, muscles)
gapNote: No A-level app on action potentials, synapses, kidney/osmoregulation or muscle contraction
title: Action Potential Lab
concept: Trigger a neurone and watch ion channels open to generate and propagate an action potential, plotting membrane potential live

strand: Chemistry: physical chemistry (atomic structure, energetics, kinetics, equilibria, thermodynamics, electrochemistry)
gapNote: Local KS5 file lists this in full (Hess, Maxwell-Boltzmann, Kc/Kp, Born-Haber, electrode potentials, pH/buffers) but no app addresses it
title: Maxwell-Boltzmann Explorer
concept: Adjust temperature and catalyst and watch the distribution shift relative to activation energy, linking to rate

strand: Practical endorsement and CPAC skills (all three sciences)
gapNote: No app framed around the A-level practical endorsement, CPAC criteria, uncertainties or error propagation
title: Uncertainty and Error Lab
concept: Take repeated virtual measurements, calculate mean, uncertainty and percentage error and propagate it through a calculation

strand: Understanding the past through stories and storytelling
gapNote: No app turns a story/storybook setting into a past-vs-present exploration as the ELG specifies.
title: Story Time Traveller
concept: Project a familiar tale (e.g. a castle story); children drag characters into a 'long ago' or 'today' room and the app narrates why each clue (candles, horses, swords) shows it happened long ago.

strand: Events beyond living memory significant nationally/globally (Great Fire of London, first flight, Moon landing)
gapNote: No app teaches a named significant past event; this is a core KS1 statutory area.
title: The Great Fire of London: 5 Days
concept: Animated map of 1666 London; pupils tap to advance each day, watch the fire spread along timber houses, and reveal a Pepys diary clue explaining why it spread and what changed afterwards.

strand: Local history: significant events, people and places in the locality
gapNote: No app supports local-history enquiry; the famous-people app is national/international, not local.
title: Our Place Then and Now
concept: Drop a class photo of a local landmark next to a historic one; a slider wipes between them and pupils tap features (gas lamps, cobbles, a vanished mill) that show how the locality has changed.

strand: British prehistory to 1066 (Stone Age to Iron Age; Romans; Anglo-Saxons & Scots; Vikings)
gapNote: No app covers the four mandatory British history units; the civilisation comparator is ancient world, not British chronology.
title: Britain Through the Ages: Invaders Map
concept: A morphing map of Britain where pupils slide through Stone Age, Roman, Anglo-Saxon and Viking layers, tapping settlements, hill forts, Hadrian's Wall and the Danelaw to see who arrived and what they left behind.

strand: A study beyond 1066 (turning point/theme in British history, e.g. WWII, railways, migration)
gapNote: No app addresses a post-1066 British theme/turning point.
title: Turning Point Investigator
concept: Pupils pick a candidate turning point (arrival of railways, Battle of Britain) and drag 'before' and 'after' images of daily life onto a balance to judge how much really changed.

strand: Medieval Britain 1066-1509: Church, state and society
gapNote: No app covers medieval substantive content (Norman Conquest, Magna Carta, Black Death, feudalism).
title: The Feudal Pyramid Builder
concept: Pupils assemble the feudal hierarchy by dragging king, barons, knights and peasants into place; pulling out a layer animates the obligations and consequences that ripple up and down.

strand: Early Modern Britain 1509-1745: Reformation, civil war, revolution
gapNote: No app covers the Reformation, English Civil War or the constitutional settlement.
title: Break with Rome: Henry VIII's Dilemma
concept: Pupils advise Henry by weighing money, heirs, faith and power; each choice animates the dissolution of the monasteries and shifts a national 'Catholic-Protestant' meter.

strand: Britain 1745-1901: ideas, political power, industry and empire (incl. slavery and abolition)
gapNote: No app covers industrialisation, empire, the transatlantic slave trade/abolition or franchise reform.
title: Industrial Town Machine
concept: Pupils drag mills, canals, railways and workers onto a growing town and watch population, pollution and child-labour dials move, then enact reforms to see the social impact.

strand: Local history study and pre-1066 consolidation (migration, changing power/landscape over time)
gapNote: No app supports a local-history enquiry or a long-arc 'theme over time' consolidation.
title: Migration to Britain: The Long View
concept: A single map animated across 2,000 years; pupils tap waves of arrivals (Romans, Saxons, Huguenots, Windrush) and chart continuity and change in who came and why.

strand: World history study and its interconnections (e.g. Mughal India, Qing China, Russian empires)
gapNote: No app covers a non-British world-history society at KS3.
title: Empires Connected
concept: Pupils explore a chosen world empire (Mughal India or Qing China) and draw trade, idea and conflict links to Europe, revealing global interconnection rather than isolated history.

strand: Period study (50+ years of national/international history, e.g. Germany 1890-1945, American West)
gapNote: No app teaches a substantive GCSE period study; existing apps are concept tools only.
title: Weimar to Nazi Germany: Power Tracker
concept: Students slide through 1918-1934 watching economy, votes and street violence interact; each pause unlocks the key event and lets them test 'how did Hitler come to power?' causes.

strand: Thematic study across centuries (Health/Medicine, Power and the People, Crime and Punishment, Migration)
gapNote: No app traces a long thematic development (a core GCSE element worth 30-40%).
title: Medicine Through Time Timeline
concept: A scrollable c1250-present timeline where students drag breakthroughs (Galen, germ theory, NHS) into place and a 'factor lens' highlights what drove or held back progress.
altIdeas: Power and the People: Protest Ladder

strand: Non-Examined Assessment: independent historical investigation
gapNote: No app scaffolds the NEA (question formulation, 100-year context, managing sources and historiography across ~4,000 words).
title: NEA Question Forge
concept: Students draft an investigation question; the app stress-tests it for debate, source availability, 100-year context and 'to what extent' framing, then builds a research and word-budget plan.

strand: Similarities and differences between this country and other places/communities
gapNote: No app compares life here with life in contrasting countries or cultural communities.
title: Around the World Storybox
concept: Tap two homes on a globe (e.g. a UK street and a contrasting country) and a split-screen shows children, homes, food and weather to drag-match same/different.

strand: Place knowledge: comparing a UK area with a contrasting non-European area
gapNote: No app compares a small UK locality with a contrasting non-European locality.
title: Here and There Compare
concept: Side-by-side photo-map of a UK village and a contrasting locality; pupils drag features into a same/different Venn diagram.

strand: Locational knowledge: UK counties/cities/regions, Europe, North and South America
gapNote: No app builds locational knowledge of UK counties/cities or maps Europe and the Americas.
title: Map the Americas
concept: Drag countries, capitals and physical features (Andes, Amazon, Rockies) onto blank continent maps with instant accuracy feedback.

strand: Latitude, longitude, hemispheres, tropics, time zones
gapNote: No app covers the global grid (Equator, tropics, Greenwich Meridian, time zones).
title: Lines on the Globe
concept: Rotate a 3D Earth; toggle Equator, tropics and Prime Meridian on, then drag a clock to any longitude to see local time change by 15 degrees per hour.

strand: Human geography: settlements, land use, economic activity, trade and resources
gapNote: No app addresses settlements, land use, trade or resource distribution at KS2.
title: Build a Settlement
concept: Place houses, shops, farms and a river crossing on a landscape and watch the model explain why settlements grow where they do.

strand: Locational knowledge: globally significant places, terrestrial and marine
gapNote: No app builds a mental map of globally significant places, oceans and marine features at KS3.
title: World in Layers
concept: Toggle thematic overlays (relief, climate, oceans, cities) on a world map to see how physical and human patterns line up.

strand: Resource geography: energy, food and water security
gapNote: No app addresses resource distribution, security or the energy mix.
title: Energy Mix Balancer
concept: Slide a country's energy sources to meet demand while watching cost, emissions and security gauges respond.

strand: Geographical skills and fieldwork: GIS, OS maps, data analysis, enquiry
gapNote: No app teaches OS map interpretation, GIS or the enquiry/data-handling cycle at KS3.
title: OS Map Detective
concept: Interpret a live OS map: solve grid-reference, contour and distance challenges with instant marking and a relief profile tool.

strand: Resource management: food, water and energy
gapNote: No app covers resource supply, demand, insecurity or management at GCSE.
title: Water Security Simulator
concept: Manage a region's water supply against demand, drought and transfer schemes, watching scarcity and conflict indices respond.

strand: Changing places
gapNote: No app addresses place identity, representation, meaning and lived experience.
title: Place Profiler
concept: Layer demographic, economic and qualitative data plus media representations onto a place to compare insider and outsider perspectives.

strand: Special objects, symbols and artefacts (sensory exploration)
gapNote: No EYFS app for handling and naming religious artefacts.
title: Mystery Artefact Box
concept: Drag a sensory 'feely box' over screen artefacts that zoom and reveal what they are and which community uses them.

strand: Special people and leaders (Jesus, Muhammad, Guru Nanak, the Buddha)
gapNote: No app introducing key founders and exemplars and what they taught.
title: Who Taught This?
concept: Match a simple teaching or kind action to the religious leader who is known for it, with a short illustrated story reveal.

strand: Big questions and learning from religion (meaning, right and wrong)
gapNote: No KS1 app prompting age-appropriate reflection on 'big questions'.
title: Wonder Wall
concept: Children post and sort 'I wonder...' questions onto a class wall, then explore how different traditions respond.

strand: Religious values, ethics and faith in action (justice, charity, ahimsa, seva)
gapNote: No KS2 app exploring how beliefs shape values and charitable action.
title: Faith in Action
concept: Match real charitable acts to the value and tradition that inspires them (zakah, tzedakah, seva), building a 'kindness in the world' board.

strand: Non-religious worldviews (Humanism) and ultimate questions
gapNote: No KS2 app introducing Humanist responses to big questions alongside religious ones.
title: Big Question Sorter
concept: Pose questions like 'why are we here?' and sort religious and Humanist answers side by side onto a comparison board.

strand: Religious identity, belonging and religion in contemporary Britain
gapNote: No app on lived religion, multiple identities, or 'believing without belonging' in modern Britain.
title: Identity Mosaic
concept: Build composite British identities by layering faith, ethnicity, culture and practice, revealing how identity is complex and individual.

strand: The problem of evil and theodicies
gapNote: No app on the logical/evidential problem of evil, soul-making, free-will defence or process theodicy.
title: Theodicy Tester
concept: Throw natural and moral evil cases at each theodicy (Hick, Plantinga, process) and watch which claims survive and where they break down.

strand: Religious experience and religious language
gapNote: No app on typologies of experience, Swinburne's principles, or the verification/falsification and symbol/analogy debates.
title: Is This Experience Veridical?
concept: Apply credulity and testimony principles to case studies of numinous and mystical experiences and weigh naturalistic counter-explanations.

strand: Meta-ethics, conscience and free will / moral responsibility
gapNote: No app on naturalism/intuitionism/divine command, the Euthyphro dilemma, conscience, or determinism vs libertarianism.
title: Euthyphro and Beyond
concept: Navigate a branching debate on whether good is good because God commands it, then map naturalist, intuitionist and emotivist positions on a meta-ethics grid.

strand: Study of religion: sacred texts, developments in thought, religion and society/dialogue
gapNote: No app on New Testament/textual study, historical developments, secularism/pluralism, or exclusivism/inclusivism/pluralism models.
title: Pluralism Positions
concept: Place religions on an exclusivist-to-pluralist spectrum and stress-test each model against real interfaith scenarios.

strand: Songs, rhymes and number patterns in the target language
gapNote: No KS1 app uses songs/rhymes, the most powerful primary tool for internalising rhythm and intonation.
title: Action Song Builder
concept: A projected karaoke-style song (counting or greetings) with highlighted words and matching actions the class performs, with a slider to slow the tempo for new learners.

strand: Reading: understand words, phrases and simple writing; use a dictionary
gapNote: No KS2 reading-comprehension app; nothing teaches reading short texts or using a bilingual dictionary.
title: Read and Match
concept: Short illustrated sentences pupils match to pictures, with a tap-to-reveal mini dictionary so they practise looking up unknown words in context.

strand: Intercultural understanding and appreciation of stories/songs
gapNote: No KS2 app explores culture, festivals or authentic stories in the target language.
title: A Day in the Life
concept: An interactive timeline of a child's day in a French/Spanish/German town; tap moments to compare school, meals and routines with the UK, with key vocabulary surfacing in context.

strand: Speaking: initiate and develop conversations with increasing spontaneity
gapNote: No KS3 app supports interactive spontaneous speaking, coping with unexpected responses or social conventions.
title: Conversation Roulette
concept: A spinner serves a random question and an unexpected follow-up; pupils must respond live using a scaffold of connectives, with the teacher escalating spontaneity as confidence grows.

strand: Reading and translation of authentic and literary texts
gapNote: No KS3 reading-comprehension or translation app; literary text reading for KS3 is unaddressed.
title: Translate the Text
concept: A short authentic passage where pupils drag English equivalents under each chunk, with a confidence heat-map showing which structures the class found hardest.

strand: Intercultural understanding of target-language countries
gapNote: No KS3 app addresses culture, customs or authentic cultural materials.
title: Culture Compare
concept: Side-by-side interactive panels (festivals, school day, food, etiquette) for a target-language country versus the UK, with pupils predicting differences before revealing the answer.

strand: Speaking: role play, photo card and general conversation
gapNote: No app rehearses the three GCSE speaking tasks (role play with unpredictable element, photo card, general conversation).
title: Photo Card Coach
concept: Projects a GCSE-style photo card, generates likely questions, and runs a countdown for pupils to describe, give opinions and react spontaneously, then reveals model answer chunks.

strand: Cultural and literature/film engagement at GCSE
gapNote: No app supports the customs/festivals content or the optional literature/film responses at GCSE.
title: Festival Explorer
concept: An interactive deep-dive into a target-language festival or tradition with embedded authentic clips and opinion-building prompts pupils use to draft a writing response.

strand: Speaking: stimulus-card discussion and individual research project
gapNote: No app supports the A-level speaking exam (theme stimulus card debate or independent research project presentation).
title: Stimulus Debate Arena
concept: Projects an A-level theme stimulus card, generates probing examiner questions, and times an extended response while suggesting counter-arguments to push spontaneity.

strand: Singing, rhyme and musical performance
gapNote: No app links music, song or rhythm to performance for EYFS.
title: Action Song Stage
concept: Project a nursery rhyme with tappable action icons; children perform the matching gesture as each line lights up, building a whole-class performance.

strand: Responding to and evaluating performance
gapNote: No KS2 app helps pupils give structured feedback on a peer performance.
title: Two Stars and a Wish
concept: After watching a clip the class drags strengths and one improvement onto a shared board, building a simple evaluation vocabulary.

strand: Vocal and physical performance skills
gapNote: No KS3 app systematically builds voice (projection, tone, pace) and physicality (posture, gesture, gait).
title: Voice and Body Lab
concept: Sliders for pitch, pace, projection and posture transform an on-screen actor; students match settings to a character brief.

strand: Design and technical theatre (set, lighting, sound, costume)
gapNote: No app addresses the design/technical option (lighting, sound, set, costume) assessed across GCSE specs.
title: Tech Theatre Designer
concept: Drag lighting states, sound cues, set pieces and costume choices onto a scene and see/justify how each shapes mood and meaning.

strand: Theatre history, genres and styles in context
gapNote: No app maps theatrical movements (Greek, Naturalism, Epic, Absurd, Physical) and their conventions and context.
title: Theatre Timeline Explorer
concept: An interactive timeline where clicking a movement reveals its conventions, key plays and a worked staging example for comparison.

strand: Making relationships and playing with others
gapNote: No EYFS app on sharing, turn-taking and joining in play.
title: Sharing the Sandpit
concept: Animated scenario where two characters both want one toy; class votes on what to do next and sees each choice play out (turn-taking, asking, sharing).

strand: Sense of belonging, routines and being safe
gapNote: No EYFS app on classroom belonging, daily routines or knowing safe adults.
title: Our Day Picture Timeline
concept: Drag-and-drop visual timetable the class orders together each morning to build routine, security and belonging.

strand: Families and people who care for me
gapNote: No app on different family structures and who cares for us at KS1.
title: All Our Families
concept: Interactive gallery of varied family set-ups; class sorts what families have in common (love, care, keeping safe) by dragging cards onto a shared chart.

strand: Being safe, privacy and asking for help
gapNote: No KS1 app on the underwear rule, secrets vs surprises, or who/how to tell.
title: Tell a Trusted Adult
concept: Branching what-would-you-do scenes (a worry, a secret that feels wrong); class chooses to keep it in or tell, and sees the relief of telling.

strand: Healthy bodies: hygiene, sleep, healthy eating and physical activity
gapNote: No KS1 health-education app on basic hygiene, sleep or healthy eating.
title: Build a Healthy Day
concept: Drag activities (teeth-brushing, fruit, play, sleep) onto a 24-hour wheel; the wheel lights up green as a balanced healthy day is built.

strand: Internet and screens: safe and kind online
gapNote: No KS1 app introducing basic online safety and telling an adult about online worries.
title: Smart Tap Tablet
concept: Simulated tablet shows pop-ups and messages; class decides tap or stop and tell, learning the first rules of staying safe online.

strand: Physical health: healthy eating, exercise, sleep, drugs/alcohol awareness
gapNote: No KS2 app on diet, exercise, sleep or first awareness of drugs, alcohol and tobacco.
title: Fuel Your Body
concept: Build a balanced plate and weekly activity plan; the on-screen avatar's energy and mood respond to the choices made.

strand: Changing adolescent body and puberty
gapNote: No KS2 app on the statutory puberty/changing body content.
title: Growing and Changing
concept: Age-appropriate interactive timeline of physical and emotional puberty changes; class sorts true/false myths and matches changes to self-care.

strand: Physical health: drugs, alcohol, tobacco/vaping and lifestyle
gapNote: No app on risk and effects of drugs, alcohol, smoking and vaping at KS3.
title: Risk and Reality
concept: Interactive risk-meter where the class weighs short and long-term effects of substances and rehearses refusal and exit strategies.

strand: Identity, diversity and respect for difference
gapNote: No app on identity, protected characteristics or valuing diversity.
title: Identity Iceberg
concept: Class builds an iceberg of visible and hidden identity for a character then their own, challenging stereotypes and assumptions live.

strand: Living in the wider world: money, careers and economic wellbeing
gapNote: No app on budgeting, financial decisions, careers or economic wellbeing.
title: First Budget Simulator
concept: Class manages a teenager's monthly money against needs, wants and surprise costs, seeing the consequences of each spending choice.

strand: Intimate and sexual relationships, consent, contraception and sexual health
gapNote: No app covers the statutory KS4 RSE content on consent, contraception, STIs and healthy intimate relationships.
title: Consent and Pressure Scenarios
concept: Realistic branching situations where the class identifies consent, coercion and the law, and rehearses clear communication.

strand: Substances, risk and personal safety
gapNote: No KS4 app on drugs, alcohol, addiction, risk-taking and personal safety.
title: Risk Trade-Off Engine
concept: Class weighs decisions involving substances, peer pressure and personal safety on an interactive risk-reward model with realistic consequences.

strand: Economic wellbeing, careers and post-16 pathways
gapNote: No app on careers, post-16 choices, financial capability or work readiness.
title: Pathways Planner
concept: Interactive map of post-16 routes (A-level, T-level, apprenticeship, work) where the class explores how choices link to careers and earnings.

strand: Adult relationships, consent and family/parenting choices
gapNote: No post-16 app on adult relationships, ongoing consent or family/parenting decisions.
title: Healthy Adult Relationships
concept: Case-study scenarios on commitment, conflict and consent in adult relationships, with the class debating and ranking responses.

strand: Financial capability and economic wellbeing for adulthood
gapNote: No app on credit, debt, tax, tenancy or adult budgeting at KS5.
title: Adulting Finance Simulator
concept: Class runs a young adult's finances across a year (rent, payslips, credit, unexpected bills), seeing how decisions build or erode security.

strand: Personal safety, online life and informed citizenship
gapNote: No KS5 app on adult online risks (scams, misinformation, data), personal safety or rights and responsibilities.
title: Spot the Scam
concept: Live stream of adult-world messages (phishing, fraud, fake news) the class judges and reports, building real-world digital resilience.
